mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.3pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply-side bottlenecks that force corporate margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes do not predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations remain anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL crosses the 3.5% threshold, or the signal fails to predict returns when the Fed maintains a dovish stance.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and rising unemployment indicates labor force participation deterioration that suppresses wage growth and corporate revenue, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE increases simultaneously
failure_condition: The joint divergence condition produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect immediate construction sector demand and wealth effects that boost consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST changes fail to predict positive 5-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when mortgage rates exceed 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a low-inflation environment confirms sustainable capacity expansion that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases during low-inflation expansions, or the signal fails when capacity utilization exceeds 85 percent.
